00:12:50	Justin Traglia:	https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5094
00:15:45	Barnabas:	How much data duplication are we talking about per slot?
00:15:58	Potuz:	the max can be large
00:17:11	Nico Flaig:	we can remove them from the envelope though but requires engine api changes
00:17:22	Justin Traglia:	Replying to "How much data duplic..."

Theoretically, ~1.5 MiB of requests. With today’s gas limit, ~500 KiB. That would require a 2500 batched deposits.
00:17:51	Justin Traglia:	Replying to "How much data duplic..."

But the average case is way, way smaller. IIRC.
00:19:17	Justin Traglia:	I don’t think we can lower the deposit request limit, because deposits are not queued. Could be wrong though.
00:19:31	Nico Flaig:	I think we can Justin
00:19:40	Justin Traglia:	Reacted to "I think we can Justi..." with 👀
00:19:50	Justin Traglia:	Replying to "I think we can Justi..."

00:20:13	Barnabas:	Replying to "How much data duplic..."

00:20:25	Justin Traglia:	Replying to "How much data duplic..."

Yup, it’s not sustainable.
00:21:37	Barnabas:	Replying to "How much data duplic..."

whats the duplicated data on a slot where there are no deposits?

00:23:46	Raúl Kripalani:	The hope is to continue increasing the gas limit over time
00:23:57	Parithosh Jayanthi:	Blocks are also often built by builders and they may not respect this particular limit if the block with deposits is more profitable
00:24:16	Toni Wahrstätter:	Instead of putting an arbitrary limit on the EL side, we should just price it such that, with a given gas limit, it stays in a range the CL is fine with.
00:24:16	Parithosh Jayanthi:	(They often don’t use vanilla clients to build blocks)
00:24:23	Parithosh Jayanthi:	Reacted to "Instead of putting an arbitrary limit on the EL side, we should just price it such that, with a given gas limit, it stays in a range the CL is fine with." with 👍

00:39:06	Raúl Kripalani:	Replying to "edge cases always involve reorgs and proposing on top of a previous payload"

This is the risk, but the problem is reduced to “the parent MUST be available”, so if clients are decently designed, there shouldn’t edge case handling in code
00:40:11	Potuz:	Replying to "edge cases always in..."
